fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency response effectiveness to the property could involve widening the single access point and ensuring it remains unobstructed to facilitate swift entry for emergency vehicles. Strategically thinning or trimming the dense vegetation, particularly near the main building and the driveway, could improve responder visibility and ease navigation. Additionally, ensuring that the driveway provides adequate space for maneuverability around the main building could support smoother staging and operational activities. Clearly marked and maintained pathways around the landscaped area would further enable efficient movement and access, while ensuring that perimeter fencing includes easily accessible gates can enhance emergency operations and expedite response times.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing the distance between tree clusters and the structure, along with managing vegetation growth immediately adjacent to the building, may bolster defensible space and reduce the threat of fire spread to the structure. Pruning or removing some of the scattered trees within the vegetated perimeter could decrease fuel continuity and further improve defensibility. It is advisable to maintain the open areas around access routes and manage vegetation along these pathways, supporting effective firefighter access and suppression operations. Regular trimming and maintenance of the vegetation bordering the driveway could help ensure safe and efficient passage. By sustaining a focus on defensible space and reducing fuel continuity, the property can support long-term wildfire resilience and preparedness.
